search_queries_used: site:bsaci.org non-allergic rhinitis guideline triggers medicines hormonal occupational examination testing 2017
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic idiopathic, vasomotor, drug-induced, hormonal, occupational, or inflammatory subtype
automatic antihistamine, steroid, anticholinergic, decongestant, testing, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Non-allergic rhinitis is heterogeneous and requires exclusion and clinician interpretation.
No medicine, test, treatment, procedure, referral, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children or adults with clinician-suspected non-allergic rhinitis; pregnancy, occupational disease, systemic vasculitis, drug effects, structural obstruction, and infection require additional pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary, allergy, respiratory, occupational, or ENT assessment.
UAE_applicability: BSACI guidance requires UAE occupational, environmental, pregnancy, prescribing, allergy-testing, ENT, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official BSACI 2017 exact history, examination, and investigation sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2017 revised edition remains listed by BSACI; material recency uncertainty remains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact BSACI history section supports onset, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, irritants, medicines, hormonal and occupational context, impact, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without assigning a non-allergic subtype.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact examination section supports clinician-performed general, nasal, septal, secretion, crusting, polyp, and throat assessment without auto-populating findings.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact testing context supports review of existing specialist information and clinician-entered plan or follow-up without automatically ordering allergy tests or treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Trigger pattern, allergy testing, medicines, pregnancy, occupation, smell, obstruction, discharge, examination, systemic features, subtype, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
